meta.title: Naudokis Vendors, Recipients, Cookies, and SDK Privacy Appendix

toc.label: Service providers, recipients, and their roles

blocks.md: This appendix forms part of the Naudokis Privacy Policy and must be interpreted together with the main Policy.

blocks.text: 10. Service providers, recipients, and their roles

blocks.md: We share data only to the extent necessary for the purposes described in this Policy. A recipient's privacy role depends on the specific function, contract, and legal obligations. Where a recipient acts as our Processor, it processes data according to our instructions and a data processing agreement. Where a recipient independently determines Processing purposes or has independent legal obligations, it acts as an independent Controller.

head: Recipient or category
Purpose
Typical privacy role
rows: AWS and related infrastructure services
hosting, storage, authentication, logs, email or SMS, queues, security, and infrastructure
Processor according to Naudokis instructions
Stripe or Stripe Connect
payments, card and payment-method processing, authorization, capture, refunds, payouts, payment Disputes, KYC, sanctions, fraud prevention, taxes, and financial compliance

Independent Controller for regulated payment, KYC, sanctions, fraud, tax, and Dispute purposes; Processor or service provider only for those technical operations performed according to Naudokis instructions
Didit
identity verification, document or identity check, liveness or document signals, security and compliance signals
Processor when acting according to Naudokis instructions; independent Controller only to the extent it has independent legal or security obligations under contract and its own privacy notice
OpenAI or other AI service providers
AI Listing descriptions, moderation support, support response drafts, or other AI functions where enabled or where you use them
Processor or service provider when used according to Naudokis instructions; independent Controller only in cases where the provider itself determines separate Processing purposes under its own terms
Expo
push notifications and technical mobile App services
Processor or service provider according to the nature of the integration
Postit.lt
server-side address search, Lithuanian address normalization, location selection
Usually Processor according to Naudokis instructions
Google (Google Places API)
server-side address autocomplete and details of the selected address
Role depends on the specific Google terms and integration; data is transferred only to the extent necessary for the address function
OpenStreetMap Foundation (Nominatim)
server-side reverse geocoding (coordinates -> administrative unit)
Separate recipient or service provider; only coordinates are transferred without direct account identifiers, to the extent possible
Apple, Google, and Facebook
social login where you choose such method
Often independent Controllers for their own authentication ecosystems; Naudokis receives only the attributes necessary for login according to the User's choice and the provider's terms
European Commission VIES system
business VAT identification number check where required for a B2B, invoicing, or tax-compliance function
Public service; Controllers are the European Commission and Member States according to applicable rules
Legal, tax, accounting, audit, compliance, support, and professional advisers
legal, accounting, tax, audit, compliance, and Dispute matters
Usually independent Controllers according to professional obligations; in some cases Processors under contract
Courts, law enforcement, tax, consumer rights, data protection, and other authorities
legal requests, regulatory compliance, Disputes, investigations
Independent Controllers under law
Payment networks, banks, and financial institutions
payments, chargebacks, Disputes, fraud prevention
Independent Controllers or separate recipients under financial-sector rules

blocks.md: We enter into data processing agreements or apply equivalent contractual safeguards with service providers that act as our Processors, including the European Commission standard contractual clauses where data is transferred outside the European Economic Area. Before starting to use a new Processor for Personal Data Processing, we enter into such contracts before the relevant Processing begins.

If a recipient acts as an independent Controller, its Processing is subject to its privacy notices, statutory obligations, and contractual terms. In such a case, Naudokis transfers only the data necessary for the specific function, legal obligation, payment, verification, Dispute, or security purpose.

blocks.text: 22.2 Appendix B: summary of vendors, recipients, and roles

blocks.md: Naudokis uses third-party vendors only to the extent necessary for a specific function. A vendor's role depends on the function, contract, and legal requirements.

Where a vendor acts as a Processor, it processes data according to Naudokis instructions and contract. Where a vendor acts as an independent Controller, it independently determines certain Processing purposes and means, for example for payment regulation, KYC, fraud prevention, sanctions, or its own legal obligations.

head: Vendor or recipient
Typical function
Typical role
Note
rows: Stripe and Stripe Connect
Payments, payouts, KYC, payment Disputes, fraud and sanctions checks
Often independent Controller for regulated payment, KYC, AML, sanctions, Dispute, and tax purposes; in some technical functions may act under contractual roles
Stripe notices and terms may apply to the User
Didit
Identity, age, or document verification
Role is determined by contract and function; Naudokis retains only necessary results and audit information, not a permanent archive of raw documents, unless this is necessary and lawful
Some document or biometric-check data may be processed by Didit according to its technology and legal obligations
AWS
Infrastructure, data storage, logs
Usually Processor or infrastructure provider
Main region is stated in the Policy, with possible limited transfers under safeguards
OpenAI or other AI vendors
AI assistance for Listing texts, support, classification, or moderation support signals where the function is enabled
Depends on the specific service configuration and contract
Not used for sensitive or unnecessary purposes without an appropriate basis; human review applies to significant decisions where needed
Google, Apple, or social-login providers
Login, authentication, device or account functions
Usually independent Controllers for their own account and security purposes
Their privacy notices apply
Google Places, OpenStreetMap or Nominatim, or other location providers
Address search, normalization, approximate location, geocoding
Depends on the vendor and function
Only data necessary for the specific function is transferred, where possible without direct identifiers
Expo, Sentry, CloudWatch, and similar technical vendors
Push notifications, diagnostics, errors, security, technical stability
Usually Processors or technical service providers according to function
Strictly necessary or diagnostic data is limited according to need
Public authorities, courts, law enforcement, STI, Consumer or data protection authorities
Legal obligations, requests, Disputes, DAC7, taxes, investigations
Independent recipients or authorities
Data is provided where required by legislation or where there is a lawful basis

blocks.md: Naudokis maintains an internal vendor register documenting the vendor name, function, data categories, role, contractual basis, international transfer mechanism, retention principles, and production activation status. The public Policy must correspond to the vendors actually used.

Before enabling a new production vendor that processes Personal Data, Naudokis must assess its role, data categories, security measures, contractual basis, international transfer mechanism, and whether this Policy or the public vendor list needs to be updated.

If a vendor or function mentioned in the Policy is not actually active in production, data is not provided to that vendor until the function is enabled and the necessary compliance assessment has been performed.

Naudokis has the right to publish a separate public vendor or subprocessor list or include it in this Policy. Such list must be aligned with actual production vendors and reviewed regularly, especially when payment, identity verification, analytics, diagnostics, AI, mapping, notification, or communication tools are enabled.

blocks.text: 22.3 Appendix C: Cookies, SDKs, local storage, and mobile technologies

blocks.md: Naudokis operates primarily as a mobile App, and therefore instead of traditional website Cookies it may use local device storage, SDK identifiers, session tokens, push-notification tokens, diagnostic events, security logs, and similar technologies.

Strictly necessary technologies are used for authentication, session, account security, fraud prevention, payment flow, saving language and settings, technical delivery of push notifications, error detection, and Platform stability. These technologies are used on the basis of contract performance, legitimate interest, or legal obligation where separate consent is not required.

Non-essential product analytics, marketing, attribution, remarketing, or similar technologies are used only where there is an appropriate legal basis, including consent where required by law. If the User refuses non-essential analytics or marketing, that refusal alone does not prevent use of the main Platform functions, unless a specific additional function objectively depends on the selected technology.
